role: Senior Frontend Engineer (mixed: product + client delivery)
description: Reference mixed role: internal product work plus a dedicated client engagement, with measurable outcomes and strong ownership.
highlights: Owned design system + app shell used across 3 internal teams; reduced UI duplication by ~30%
Client delivery: shipped B2B workflows and improved LCP by ~20% on top pages
Set up regression checks + release checklist to reduce production incidents

description: Placed by Ritech at Trackforce (Canada) to deliver frontend features for a workforce management platform.
highlights: Shipped scheduling and incident workflow improvements for an enterprise platform
Improved UI stability by standardizing shared components and tightening QA feedback loops

description: Moved from client delivery to Ritech’s owned AI product, building reviewer workflows and audit-friendly UI.
highlights: Built AI review workflows (history, approvals, diffs) with clear error states and retries
Improved UX for long-running tasks and reduced support escalations via better feedback patterns

description: Joined the product team and delivered core UI features while establishing frontend patterns for a growing codebase.
highlights: Shipped core workflows and improved UI performance on large views
Improved cross-team integration by aligning API contracts and error handling patterns

description: Led frontend delivery for product modules and desktop surfaces; improved architecture and release stability.

Improved maintainability by standardizing patterns for state, forms, and error handling

description: Promotion track: mentored engineers, drove architecture decisions, and aligned delivery across squads.

description: Consulting delivery across Angular/Ionic modules for multiple clients.

description: Led frontend delivery and supported two parallel tracks (internal platform + client delivery).

description: Part-time role shipping mobile features with cross-platform tooling.

description: Converted from part-time to full-time to ship a major release and stabilize performance.

description: Client-focused delivery across web + mobile modules with frequent releases.

description: Delivered CMS sites and internal tooling while owning deployments and reliability.
